title: Fix associative array size() constraint generating invalid resize() call
body:
## Summary

When a constraint block contains `assoc_array.size() == N`, Verilator generates C++ code calling `resize()` on `VlAssocArray`, which does not have that method. Elaboration succeeds but C++ compilation fails. Per IEEE 1800-2017 §7.9, associative arrays are key-value maps, not resizable sequential containers.

## Current Behavior

- `constraint c { data.size() == 2; }` on a `rand int data[string]` passes Verilator elaboration
- C++ compilation fails: `'class VlAssocArray<std::string, unsigned int>' has no member named 'resize'`
- Generated function name `__VnoInFunc_c_resize_constrained_array` confirms dynamic-array resize logic is incorrectly applied

## Expected Behavior

- Either: associative array size constraints are supported correctly
- Or: Verilator reports an unsupported-feature error at elaboration time (not a C++ compilation failure)

## Source

- planvtech/PlanV_Verilator_Feature_Tests#52
